subject: Anthropology
title: Bipedalism
description: A micro-lesson that makes Bipedalism usable.

hook.text: Walking on two legs seems simple. But it required reshaping the spine, pelvis, knees, and feet entirely.

buildup.text: Bipedalism — walking upright on two legs — is the earliest defining trait of the hominin lineage.

discovery.text: It freed the hands for carrying food and tools, and it's far more energy-efficient for long-distance travel.

twist.text: Bipedalism came before big brains. Our ancestors walked upright millions of years before they thought complex thoughts.

climax.text: Standing up changed everything. It reshaped our skeleton, our diet, our tools, and eventually our minds.

punchline.text: The first human act was standing up.

quiz.question: What is significant about bipedalism in human evolution?
options: It was the earliest defining hominin trait
It made humans slower than apes
